# AgentOrbit

> AI agent observability without code changes. Proxy between your agent and LLM provider.

AgentOrbit is a proxy and observability layer that sits between AI agents and LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, and any OpenAI-compatible API). Agents point their `base_url` at AgentOrbit — it transparently proxies requests, captures every span, groups them into sessions, detects failure patterns, and serves a real-time dashboard.

## How it works

1. Create an account and get your AgentOrbit API key
2. Change `base_url` in your agent config to the AgentOrbit proxy endpoint
3. Every request is traced — sessions, failures, and metrics appear instantly

No SDK required. Two config lines. ~59ms overhead per request.

## Key features

- **Session Timeline**: See every LLM request in a session with status, tokens, cost, and duration
- **Failure Clusters**: Discover recurring error patterns across agent sessions
- **Request Categories**: See which tasks users give the agent and which underperform
- **Multi-provider**: OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, and any OpenAI-compatible API in one dashboard
- **Failure Rate Alerts**: Get notified when your agent starts failing more than usual
- **Readable by non-engineers**: Session timelines are understandable without knowing prompts or token counts

## Compatibility

Works with any agent that uses a standard LLM SDK with configurable `base_url`:
- OpenClaw, n8n, Cursor, Crew AI — via URL change

Does NOT work with closed-SDK agents:
- Claude Code (hardcoded Anthropic endpoints)
- GitHub Copilot (proprietary infrastructure)

## Pricing

- **Free**: 1,000 spans/month, 3 members, 1 project, 30 days retention
- **Pro** (beta): 100,000 spans/month, 20 members, 10 projects, LLM narratives, alerts
- **Self-host**: Unlimited everything, your infrastructure, open-source (Docker Compose)
